questions.prompt: Grammar — Complete the past simple: "Yesterday my sister ___ a cake for the party."
questions.explanation: bake is a regular verb ending in -e, so we add only -d for the past: baked. With "yesterday" we need the past, so "bakes"/"bake" (present) and "baking" (the -ing form, which needs be) are all wrong.

questions.prompt: Spelling — Choose the correct -ing form: "Look! The dog ___ across the field."
questions.explanation: run has one short vowel + one consonant, so we double the n before -ing: running. "is runing" forgets to double the n, "is runeing" adds a wrong e, and "is run" drops the -ing the continuous needs.

questions.prompt: Vocabulary — Which word is the odd one out?
questions.explanation: Apple, banana and orange are all fruits, but a carrot is a vegetable, so it is the odd one out. Sort by meaning: the set looks like "food", but only the fruits belong together.

questions.prompt:
Reading — Read: "Karim got up early on Sunday. He went to the bakery and bought fresh bread for breakfast."
Where did Karim go?
questions.explanation: The text says "He went to the bakery", so that is the answer. A reading answer must come from the text: school, cinema and beach are real places but are never mentioned, so they cannot be correct.

questions.prompt: Grammar — Future: "I've already decided — next summer I ___ visit my uncle in Sfax." (a plan)
questions.explanation: A plan you already decided uses be going to + base verb: I am going to visit. "will to" is never correct (will takes no to), "going to" alone drops the be (am), and "go to" is the present, not a future plan.

questions.prompt: Spelling — Choose the correctly spelled past form: "He ___ very hard for the test last week."
questions.explanation: study ends in consonant + y, so the y becomes -ied in the past: studied. "studyed" keeps the y wrongly, "studed" drops a letter, and "studies" is the present he/she/it form, not the past.
